title: The Paradoxical Atrium
setting: A vast indoor garden where rain falls upward from the soil into the glass canopy.
narrative_introduction: The instrument opens on a vast indoor garden where rain falls upward from the soil into the glass canopy. Do not trust your first glance.

mechanics.observation: Locked-off 2-second cinematic moment. Painter's hand lifts the brush, pauses midway, and turns toward the window before lowering the brush untouched.
mechanics.memory: Spatial fragment placement across the easel desk. Drag sensory fragments (wet linseed oil, paused sable brush, golden window shaft, half-mixed cerulean blue, color swatch note, prism crystal, linen texture) to their exact coordinates.
mechanics.discovery: Frozen moment exploration. Tap objects on the easel and window sill to attune. Attunements reveal the exact optical angle of the sun and the reflection inside the crystal prism.
mechanics.evidence: Archive entry builds from placed fragments + attunements. Reveals hidden truth: the painter paused not from block, but because the window prism refracted a rainbow across the canvas.
mechanics.reflection: Iris voice synthesizes what player noticed. Archive entry: 'The Unfinished Canvas'. No score or stars—only what was carried.

environment.location: artist studio loft, window ledge, afternoon

lighting.primary: warm golden hour direct sun, camera-right, 25 degrees
lighting.fill: soft ambient bounce from white plaster walls
lighting.key_detail: crystal prism on sill refracting a spectrum onto the blank canvas

camera.depth_of_field: shallow, canvas surface and brush sharp
camera.color_grade: rich warm golden, deep amber shadows, vibrant cyan/orange spectrum

animation.hand: lifts (0.7s) -> pauses looking right (0.8s) -> lowers brush (0.5s) = 2.0s
animation.steam: faint mineral spirits vapor rising slowly
animation.dust_mote: golden dust motes swirling in the window beam
animation.light: sun beam shifts imperceptibly across the sill

ambient_details: sable hair brush resting on glass palette
crystal glass prism sitting on wooden sill
half-squeezed tube of cerulean blue oil paint
sketched color notes pinned to easel edge
linen canvas with faint charcoal underdrawing
glass jar of clear linseed oil

audio.base_layer: quiet courtyard birdsong, distant breeze, wooden floor creak
audio.focus_layer: brush bristle whisper, glass jar clink, gentle inhalation

subtle: sun shaft hits prism on sill
deep: color notes on easel match the prism spectrum
painter's wrist turns toward the light
intentionally_missed_first_view: refracted rainbow exactly aligns with charcoal sketch
blue paint tube is uncapped and wet

reconstruction.iris_prompt: Reconstruct the painter's desk. Place what caught your attention.

attunements.reveals: refraction angle 42 degrees, splitting 514nm golden light into pure cyan and amber spectrum
attunements.narrative: The soil is completely dry, meaning the 'rain' is actually gas condensing on the canopy.

attunements.reveals: charcoal underdrawing traces the exact path of the prism rainbow across the linen
sable tip contains wet cerulean paint ready to trace the refracted light
handwritten margin note: 'Wait for the 5:14 light to split the room'

investigation.iris_intervention: You have seen what inspired the pause. The canvas was not abandoned; it was waiting for the light.

revelation.iris_response: You noticed the paused brush. You noticed the crystal prism on the sill. You noticed the spectrum beam striking the blank linen. The painter did not stop from doubt. They stopped because the light completed the composition. This is what it means to Witness.
revelation.truth_unlocked: It wasn't water. It was a heavier-than-air vapor. Tested state-of-matter observation and directionality.

archive_mapping.title: The Paradoxical Atrium
archive_mapping.iris_note: The brush paused not in hesitation, but in reverence for the light across the linen.
